source.title: Accident Investigation Report - VR-369 Alpha Platform Pressure Release Injury

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Vermilion Area Block 369

event.incident_type: Uncontrolled pressure release from well tree
actions_taken: First aid provided with eye wash bottles, clean bandages and Advil
Medevac flight requested and arrived at 1:20 AM
IP transported to Lafayette General Hospital at 1:34 AM
Interviews conducted with personnel and contractor employees
event.summary: On April 4, 2024, at approximately 10:30 PM, a Cajun Cutters welder was injured when unexpected gas pressure was released from the top of well A-15 tree during removal of a tree cap adapter flange. The worker was impacted in the left eye and left side of face by the pressure release. The incident occurred during temporary production piping reconfiguration work for pipeline depressurization. The worker had been on duty for 15.5 hours. Lockout/Tagout procedures were not properly implemented to ensure positive isolation of all well tree valves. The injured person required medical evacuation and sustained partial permanent hearing loss and eye injuries requiring ongoing treatment.
key_phrases: trapped pressure from the valve cavity was released
unexpected release of pressure from the top of the well tree
Lockout/Tagout was not implemented to ensure positive isolation
operator with no previous platform specific experience
IP was on duty for 15.5 hours
No safe working surfaces were provided

hazards.name: Pressurized hydrocarbon gas in well tree

threats.name: Inadequate isolation of well tree valves
Opening well tree without verification of zero energy state

controls.name: Lockout/Tagout of well tree valves
controls.barrier_role: Prevent unauthorized valve operation and ensure isolation

supporting_text: Lockout/Tagout was not implemented to ensure positive isolation of all A-15 well tree valves
According to the paperwork provided, Lockout/Tagout of the A-15 lower manual master valve was not installed until after the incident occurred
No record of Lockout/Tagout was provided to the BSEE investigators for the automatic surface safety valve

controls.name: Written procedures for valve isolation and pressure verification
controls.barrier_role: Provide clear instructions for safe work execution

supporting_text: Renaissance was not able to provide such documents

There were no written procedures provided to the BSEE investigators, nor written instructions provided to personnel that would specify which valves had been isolated and ensure all pressure had been removed

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for well tree work
controls.barrier_role: Identify and mitigate hazards before work begins

supporting_text: Cajun Cutters' JSA discussed 'not following procedure and stored energy as potential hazards' and '100% containment and checking for pressure' as steps to eliminate hazards
The scaffold builder (GSSI), who was assisting with the job, was not listed on the JSA
No site specific JSA was prepared discussing job steps or body positioning

controls.name: Pressure verification before opening well tree
controls.barrier_role: Verify zero energy state before breaking containment

supporting_text: At approximately 2:27 PM the platform was shut in by the platform operators and pressure from the tie-in points was bled to zero. After verifying the pressure was removed, Cajun Cutters removed a section of pipeline piping
Witnesses stated that the A-15 well had 800 psig of pressure at the top of the well tree before the pressure was bled to zero above the Surface Safety Valve (SSV)
Renaissance reported that trapped gas pressure was released from a valve cavity but did not identify which valve the pressure was released from

controls.name: Surface Safety Valve (SSV) - automatic
controls.barrier_role: Automatic isolation of well in emergency conditions

supporting_text: A recent Surface Controlled Sub-Surface Safety Valve (SCSSV) test was collected indicating the A-15 well had a shut-in tubing pressure of 1195 psig
Witnesses stated that the A-15 well had 800 psig of pressure at the top of the well tree before the pressure was bled to zero above the Surface Safety Valve (SSV)

controls.name: Platform operator supervision and monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Oversee work activities and ensure safe execution

supporting_text: Renaissance designated an operator with no previous platform specific experience as the sole operator on duty of the overnight shift
The night shift operator's duties included routine production operations of the platform, including maintaining the incoming production from VR-408A
The night operator arrived at VR-369A the morning of the incident
The IP stated in his interview that he had a conversation with the PIC before removing the tree cap adapter to verify that they would continue working
In more than one statement, personnel claimed to have heard a process alarm moments before the pressure release
Statements included such comments as 'The tree blew up with pressure,' 'It was so loud,' and 'I heard what sounded like a gas release @~10:30 PM. I got out of bed and got dressed.'

controls.name: Safe work platform and access
controls.barrier_role: Provide safe working position to reduce exposure

supporting_text: No safe working surfaces were provided around the entire A-15 well which contributed to the poor body positioning of the IP and helper
The IP was standing with one foot on the SSV the other on the moveable platform and the helper was standing on flowline wing valve of the A-15 well
This position placed the top of the well tree at the IP and helper's midsection with them looking down at the tree cap

controls.name: Work hour limits and fatigue management

supporting_text: The IP was on duty for 15.5 hours, from 6:00 AM until the time of the incident at approximately 10:30 PM

controls.name: First aid and emergency response
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical care after injury

supporting_text: The IP was provided first aid with eye wash bottles, clean bandages and Advil
After first aid, the decision was made to request a medevac flight

controls.name: Medical evacuation capability
controls.barrier_role: Transport injured personnel to advanced medical care

supporting_text: On April 5, 2024, at approximately 12:20 AM, the platform was notified that the medevac flight would arrive at 1:20 AM
The Medevac departed VR-369A with the IP at 1:34 AM and was flown to Lafayette General Hospital to receive emergency treatment

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) - eye protection
controls.barrier_role: Reduce severity of injury from pressure release
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense for personnel

supporting_text: an unexpected release of pressure from the top of the well tree contacted the IP's left eye and the left side of the IP's face
The IP has received treatments to remove foreign substances from the eye

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
